findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed to source control. `api_secret_key` has a plaintext default of `"super-secret-key-do-not-share"`. Because pydantic-settings only overrides this value when `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is present in the environment, any deployment that omits the variable silently uses the weak, publicly-known default. The secret is also permanently recorded in git history after this commit.
findings.suggestion: Remove the default entirely and declare the field as required (`api_secret_key: str`) so pydantic-settings raises a `ValidationError` at startup if `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is unset. Use `SecretStr` instead of `str` to prevent the value from appearing in logs or repr output: `api_secret_key: SecretStr`. Document the required env var in `.env.example` with a placeholder, not a real value.

raw_text:

Because pydantic-settings only overrides this value when `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is present in the environment, any deployment that omits the variable silently uses the weak, publicly-known default. The secret is also permanently recorded in git history after this commit.",
    "suggestion": "Remove the default entirely and declare the field as required (`api_secret_key: str`) so pydantic-settings raises a `ValidationError` at startup if `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is unset. Use `SecretStr` instead of `str` to prevent the value from appearing in logs or repr output: `api_secret_key: SecretStr`.
